page: Hetzner Cloud ccx23 is a dedicated CPU virtual server built on the x86_64 architecture using the QEMU hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical AMD EPYC Milan cores running at 2.0 GHz, with 2 threads per core. The processor includes 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. System memory consists of 16.0 GB RAM, yielding 3.91 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 160 GB SSD. The instance does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and bzip3 single-threaded decompression scoring in the top 25%. However, multi-core and memory-bound operations show limitations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core score, and software compilation benchmarks fall into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth read-only tests also rank in the bottom 25%, though write bandwidth and static web serving throughput remain in the middle 50%. Large-model LLM inference is highly constrained, with prompt processing for a medium 7B model registering in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ccx23 offers dedicated CPU resources and a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a stable option for workloads requiring predictable single-threaded CPU performance without the resource contention of shared-core models. However, the lower multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth limit its utility for heavy parallel processing or large-scale database operations. This server is suited for single-threaded processing tasks, text processing, static web serving, and lightweight application hosting.
description: Hetzner Cloud ccx23 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs from 2 physical AMD EPYC Milan cores running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, a 160 GB local SSD, and no GPU. Benchmarks show strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core and bzip3 single-threaded decompression scoring in the top 25%. Conversely, multi-core performance, memory read bandwidth, and database operations fall into the bottom 25%. This resource profile provides dedicated CPU stability with a balanced 1:4 memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it qualitatively cost-effective for workloads that do not require heavy multi-threaded scaling. It is suited for text processing, static web serving, and lightweight single-threaded applications.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx23 is an x86_64 virtual server with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 160 GB SSD, offering strong single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx23 is an x86_64 virtual server with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 160 GB SSD for single-core workloads.
tagline: Dedicated AMD EPYC Milan processor cores with balanced memory and local SSD storage for predictable single-threaded application workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs on AMD EPYC Milan architecture
16.0 GB RAM and 160 GB local SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and text processing performance
Lower multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth
Suited for static web serving and single-threaded tasks
